title: Your first select
instruction: `select` waits on multiple channels and runs whichever one is ready first. A fiction channel has "Dune" ready. Run this and see select pick the ready channel.
successMessage: Select picked fiction because it had data ready. Non-fiction was empty — so select ignored it.

title: select with default — non-blocking
instruction: Add a `default` case to make select non-blocking. If no channel has data, default runs immediately. The holds shelf is empty — add a default branch that prints "No holds available".
hint: Add default: fmt.Println("No holds available") to the select block.
successMessage: The holds shelf was empty, so default ran immediately. Non-blocking checks prevent your program from freezing.

title: Timeout — don't wait forever
instruction: `time.After(d)` returns a channel that fires after duration `d`. The catalog search takes 200ms but you want to wait max 50ms. The timeout should fire first.
hint: Add case <-time.After(50 * time.Millisecond): fmt.Println("Search timed out") — the timer wins before the search finishes.
successMessage: 50ms timer fired before the 200ms search finished. Timeouts prevent your program from hanging forever.

title: Done channel — stop gracefully
instruction: A `done` channel signals a goroutine to stop. The librarian processes returns until the library closes. Sleep 100ms, then `close(done)` to stop her gracefully.
hint: Change time.Sleep(0) to time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond). Then add close(done) on the next line.
successMessage: The librarian processed books until done was closed, then exited gracefully. The done channel pattern stops goroutines cleanly.

title: Fan-in — merge two catalog searches
instruction: Fan-in merges multiple channels into one. The `merge` function launches forwarding goroutines but they are missing. Add `go forward(fiction)` and `go forward(nonFiction)` inside merge before the wait goroutine.
hint: Add go forward(fiction) and go forward(nonFiction) inside merge, before the wait goroutine.
successMessage: Two catalog channels merged into one. Both forward goroutines dump their results onto the same output channel. Fan-in combines multiple data sources.
